zh.lifeSummary: 當Mattie Eleanor Massey於1891年10月14日出生在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林德 洛奇時，她的父親Harrison Riley Massey31歲，母親Lura Ann Bray27歲。她和Joseph Ecem Hughes至少有3個兒子和4個女兒。她於1935年住在美國阿肯色州克萊且於1940年住在美國加州柯恩朱迪希阿爾 堂史普 1。她去世於1973年10月24日享年82歲，埋葬在美國加州柯恩貝可斯費爾德格林拉 其特利 素維斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Mattie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Old German: Mighty battle maid

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Plessy vs. Ferguson
historicEvents.description: A landmark decision of the U.S. Supreme Court upholding the constitutionality of racial segregation laws for public facilities if the segregated facilities were equal in quality. It's widely regarded as one of the worst decisions in U.S. Supreme Court history.

historicEvents.title: William H. Fuller Grows 70 Acres of Rice
historicEvents.description: Rice is one Arkansas leading crops, in 1904 William H. Fuller planted 70 acres of rice, this act is what started the making rice the leading crop in Arkansas.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The Encyclopedia of Arkansas History & Culture- Rice Industry

historicEvents.title: The Sixteenth Amendment
historicEvents.description: The Sixteenth Amendment allows Congress to collect an income tax without dividing it among the states based on population.

historicEvents.title: The First Radio Station WOK Begins Broadcasting
historicEvents.description: In 1922, Harvey C. Couch Sr. started WOK the first radio station in Arkansas. After a trip to Pittsburgh and the KDKA radio he came up with the idea for Workers of Killowatts (WOK). WOK had no commercials which was nice for the listeners.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The Encyclopedia of Arkansas & Culture- WOK Radio Station

historicEvents.title: The FBI is Established
historicEvents.description: The Bureau of Investigation's name was changed to the Federal Bureau of Investigation to help citizens know that the Government is helping protect from threats both domestically and abroad.

historicEvents.title: The Civil Rights Movement Begins
historicEvents.description: The civil rights movement was a movement to enforce constitutional and legal rights for African Americans that the other Americans enjoyed. By using nonviolent campaigns, those involved secured new recognition in laws and federal protection of all Americans. Moderators worked with Congress to pass of several pieces of legislation that overturned discriminatory practices.

historicEvents.title: Troops are sent to Central High School
historicEvents.description: In September 1957, troops were sent to Central High school in Little Rock, Arkansas. The reason was that nine African American children were going to start going to this school and putting the new law of Brown v Board of Education into action. These nine children became known as the Little Rock nine.

en.lifeSummary: When Mattie Eleanor Massey was born on 14 October 1891, in De Roche, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States, her father, Harrison Riley Massey, was 31 and her mother, Lura Ann Bray, was 27. She had at least 3 sons and 4 daughters with Joseph Ecem Hughes. She lived in Clay, Arkansas, United States in 1935 and Judicial Township 15, Kern, California, United States in 1940. She died on 24 October 1973, at the age of 82, and was buried in Greenlawn Cemetery Southwest, Bakersfield, Kern, California, United States.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mattie Eleanor Massey nasceu em 14 de outubro de 1891, em De Roche, Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Harrison Riley Massey, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lura Ann Bray, tinha 27 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas com Joseph Ecem Hughes. Ela viveu em Clay, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1935 e em Judicial Township 15, Kern, Califórnia, Estados Unidos da América em 1940. Ela faleceu em 24 de outubro de 1973, com 82 anos, e foi sepultada em Greenlawn Cemetery Southwest, Bakersfield, Kern, Califórnia, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mattie Eleanor Massey родилась 14 октября 1891 г. в местности де рош, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Harrison Riley Massey было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lura Ann Bray, 27. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери от Joseph Ecem Hughes. Она жила в местности клеи, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1935 году и в местности джудисяль тоуншип 1, кирн, Калифорния, Соединенные штаты в 1940 году. Она умерла 24 октября 1973 г. в возрасте 82 лет и была похоронена в местности гринлавн кеметири соутвест, беикирсфиелд, кирн, Калифорния, Соединенные штаты.
